sentence_reviews.text: Latent state evolves according to learned transition and observation operators.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: State-space modeling references in PML/AIMA cover latent states, transition/observation operators, linear algebra, sequences, differential-equation/dynamical-system background; DLbook is less direct for modern SSM descendants.
sentence_reviews.notes: The claim is defensible by combining the cited mechanism source with dependency/background sources, but is not a single-source verbatim claim.

sentence_reviews.text: Its benefit depends on implementation, scale, data, and hardware; the same mechanism can add complexity without improving the target workload. Its direct dependencies include Differential equations, Dynamical systems & control theory.
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: Synthesized from cited node sources plus the atlas dependency context in data/atlas.json.
sentence_reviews.notes: Limitation is reviewed as a broad synthesis/tradeoff statement for the family-level node, not as a direct quotation from one source.

sentence_reviews.text: In this atlas graph, State-space transitions is modeled as depending on Differential equations, Dynamical systems & control theory and feeding downstream nodes such as Selective state-space scan, Structured state-space models (S4 family), Selective state-space models (Mamba family).
sentence_reviews.evidence_location: data/atlas.json canonical node and dependency graph; generated from node dependencies and downstream consumers.
sentence_reviews.notes: This is an explicit internal atlas-graph positioning claim, not an external-world necessity claim.
